diode rectifier: output near Vpeak minus Vf with ripple
zener regulator: output clamps at Vz
BJT common-emitter: DC bias point Vce > 0, Ic > 0
BJT differential pair: balanced outputs when inputs equal
BJT Darlington pair: high current gain
BJT push-pull: complementary output follows input
MOSFET common-source: DC bias Vds > Vgs-Vth
CMOS inverter: Vin=0 gives Vout=Vdd
CMOS NAND: truth table at analog voltages
JFET amplifier: pinch-off region operation
cascode amplifier: two-stage BJT with higher output impedance
Wilson current mirror: output current tracks reference
Widlar current source: low current output
MOSFET H-bridge: forward and reverse drive states
BJT+MOSFET mixed driver: NPN drives NMOS gate
multi-stage amplifier: three CE stages with overall gain
switched RC: charge on close, discharge on open
LRC with switch: damped oscillation after closing
relay-driven LC: relay switches between load paths
switched capacitor filter: clock-driven analog switches
SPDT source selector: toggles between two voltage sources
BJT switch with flyback diode: clamps inductive kick
MOSFET PWM into RLC: filtered DC output
crystal oscillator: oscillation builds at crystal frequency
op-amp inverting: gain equals -Rf/Rin
op-amp integrator: ramp output for DC input
555 astable: oscillation at expected frequency
SCR latch: trigger on and stays latched
triac dimmer: phase-angle AC control

diagnostic: use import to create headless facade
AND gate: place, wire, simulate, verify truth table
OR gate: place, wire, simulate, verify truth table
NOT gate: inverter
NAND gate: verify truth table
XOR gate: verify truth table
half adder: XOr for sum, And for carry (fan-out)
full adder: sum and carry for all 8 input combos
SR latch from NAND gates: set, hold, reset
D flip-flop: clock-edge triggered latch
JK flip-flop: hold, set, reset, toggle
T flip-flop 4-bit ripple counter
4-bit counter: count sequence via built-in Counter
2:1 Mux: selector routes correct input to output
4:1 Mux: 2-bit selector routes one of four inputs
Decoder: 2-bit input produces one-hot outputs
4-bit adder: multi-bit arithmetic with carry
Comparator: less, equal, greater for 4-bit values
ROM lookup: read preloaded data by address
RAM write/read: store and retrieve values
Register file: write and read registers
Tunnel wiring: signal propagates through named tunnels
Bus splitter: 8-bit to 4+4 nibbles
Priority encoder: highest active input index
File > New resets the circuit
Edit > Undo/Redo menu items exist
View > Fit to Content does not crash
